title: EFAS Flash Flood Warning Issued
description: EFAS issues first probabilistic flood alert for Rhine tributaries. Probability of exceeding 5-year return period: 65%. Sent to German federal authorities via EFFIS portal.

title: EFAS Upgrades to Formal Flood Warning
description: EFAS formal notification sent to German Federal Institute of Hydrology (BfG). Warning covers Ahr, Erft, and Rur catchments. Probability exceeding 20-year return period: >50%.

title: DWD Severe Weather Watch (Vorabinformation)
description: DWD issues advance warning for extreme precipitation in Eifel region. Expected: 100-150mm in 48 hours. Landkreise receive PDF via email.

title: DWD Extreme Weather Warning Level 4 (Unwetterwarnung)
description: Highest DWD warning level issued: >200mm rainfall expected in Eifel/Ahr within 24h. Warning distributed to Landkreise Ahrweiler, Euskirchen, and Rhein-Sieg-Kreis.

title: Müsch Gauge Exceeds Warning Level
description: First Ahr gauge to cross warning threshold (150cm). Water level rising at 15cm/hour. No automated alert system connected to this gauge.

title: Multiple Gauges Exceed Warning Levels
description: Schuld (180cm) and Altenahr (200cm) gauges cross warning thresholds. Rate of rise accelerating. Landkreis Ahrweiler activates crisis staff.

title: Schuld Gauge Exceeds Critical Level
description: Schuld gauge at 380cm and rising — exceeds all-time record (320cm from 2016). Exponential rise indicates flash flood. No automated evacuation trigger exists.

title: Altenahr Gauge Exceeds Critical Level
description: Altenahr reads 420cm — well above critical (400cm). Rising 50cm/hour. Gauge will be destroyed at ~03:00 when peak exceeds 700cm.

title: Landkreis Ahrweiler Issues First Warning via Katwarn
description: First official public warning sent via Katwarn app: 'Extreme flooding Ahr. Seek higher ground immediately.' Many residents asleep, not registered for app.

title: Cell Broadcast Evacuation Would Have Been Possible
description: Cell broadcast (DE-Alert) was not yet deployed in Germany. Would have reached all mobile phones in affected area without app registration.

title: Catastrophic Peak — Ahr Valley
description: Ahr river reaches estimated 7m+ at Altenahr. Entire valley floor inundated. Houses swept from foundations. Bridges destroyed. Gauge infrastructure lost.

title: Erftstadt-Blessem Gravel Pit Collapse
description: Floodwaters erode into active gravel quarry at Erftstadt-Blessem, creating massive sinkhole that swallows houses and roads.

title: Federal THW Deploys to Ahr Valley
description: Technisches Hilfswerk (THW) deploys major assets. Road infrastructure destroyed — many villages unreachable. Helicopter evacuations begin.

title: Steinbachtalsperre Dam Failure Feared
description: Authorities order precautionary evacuation of Swisttal-Odendorf due to risk of Steinbachtalsperre dam breach. Dam holds, but illustrates cascading risks.
